---
page-layout: article
title: "Audrey Campeau"
description: "Principal Investigator"
---

## Bio

::::: grid
::: g-col-8
Hi I'm Audrey,

I am an assistant professor in physical geography at the department of geography at the Université de Montréal. I specialize in watershed biogeochemistry and hydrology. My research focuses on headwater catchments, those that have the strongest land-water connectivity💧.

I am fascinated with data visualization and I love the endless possibilities that programming can offer. I am a strong advocate for open and FARE science.

My personal time is devoted to my three young children 🚸. I love all textile arts! If you see me wearing something woolly, I probably made it 🧶
:::

::: g-col-4
### 

![](/images/Profile%20Photo.png){fig-align="center" width="300"}
:::
:::::

#### Academic timeline

![](/images/audrey_bio.png)

## Affiliation

:::: grid
::: g-col-8
I am a member of the [Groupe de recherche interuniversitaire en limnologie (GRIL).](https://oraprdnt.uqtr.uquebec.ca/portail/gscw031?owa_no_site=543)
:::
::::

## Awards

-   [*L’Oréal – UNESCO prize for Women in Science, Sweden*](https://sverigesungaakademi.se/barn-och-samhalle/klimat-och-ai-forskning-i-fokus-for-2023-ars-mottagare-av-loreal-unesco-for-women-in-science-priset/) (2023)

-   *King Carl XVI Gustaf’s 50^th^ anniversary fund* for science, technology and the environment (2020)

-   *Alfort Prize- Best PhD in Natural Geography* from the Swedish Society for Anthropology and Geography (SSAG) (2020)

## Teaching

I teach several courses at the department including the first field course (Terrain 1 \| [GEO1315](https://admission.umontreal.ca/cours-et-horaires/cours/geo-1315/)), the watershed course (Bassins Versants \| [GEO3162](https://admission.umontreal.ca/cours-et-horaires/cours/geo-3162/)) and the ecosystem dynamics course (Dynamiques des Écosystèmes \| [GEO6100](https://admission.umontreal.ca/cours-et-horaires/cours/geo-6100/)).

:::: grid
::: g-col-8
:::
::::

You can visualise the data collected for the physical geography portion of the field courses 👇 across different editions. Those reports were produced in collaboration with students and grad-students as part of individual projects using R programming and Quarto.

:::::: grid
::: g-col-4
#### 2026 Data Viz report

![](/images/Teaching/Logo_2026.png){width="300"}

\[Coming up soon\]
:::

::: g-col-4
#### 2025 Data Viz report

![](/images/Teaching/Logo_2025.png){width="300"}

\[[Check it out here →](https://audreycampeau.github.io/GEO1315_2025_DataViz/)\]
:::

::: g-col-4
#### 2024 Data Viz report

![](/images/Teaching/Logo_2024.png){width="300"}

\[[Check it out here →](https://audreycampeau.github.io/geo-terrain/)\]
:::
::::::
